rationale: The cited paper appears theoretically relevant rather than incidental: it develops an emotion-specific affect-regulation account and explicitly argues against a pure valence view, which aligns with the citing paper’s focus on discrete positive emotions and its claim that positive emotions do not uniformly drive prosocial behavior. Given this conceptual overlap (emotion specificity vs generalized positive affect), this citation could be serving as a mechanism/theory source and should be retained for full relation judgment.
